Documentation for flash memory drivers, detailing main and alternate APIs for flash access.

# Purpose
Flash memory access in the system is managed through two distinct APIs. The primary API, defined in `flash.h` and implemented in `flash_api.c`, is used for general operations across the system. An alternate API, implemented in `cd_flash_driver.c`, is specifically designed for core dump operations and is constructed to function independently of the system state, without relying on operating system services. Both APIs are designed to be independent of the specific flash memory type, utilizing a generic low-level driver interface defined in `flash_impl.h`. These low-level drivers are tailored to specific combinations of flash memory parts and microcontroller interfaces, and they do not depend on OS services, ensuring compatibility with both the main and core dump APIs.
